front: Play: set during Carnival in Naples; subtitled 'The Banish'd Cavaliers'; Hellena and the licentious Captain Willmore; Angellica Bianca holds Willmore at gunpoint; based on Killigrew's Thomaso
Play: Lucetta seduces Blunt and drops him through a trapdoor; 'there's no sinner like a young saint'
Novel: a Coramantien prince enslaved in Suriname kills his lover Imoinda and leads a failed slave revolt before being dismembered
Play: Dr. Baliardo wants to marry his daughters to the Prince of Thunderland and the Emperor of the Moon; men disguise as aliens to trick him
back: The Emperor of the Moon (Aphra Behn)

front: Poem: the shepherd Lysander 'fell weeping in his Hands' due to impotence with Cloris; originally misattributed to the Earl of Rochester
Author: Virginia Woolf wrote that 'all women ought to let flowers fall upon' this author's tomb in Westminster Abbey's East Cloister
Author: spied for Charles II using codenames 'Agent 160' and 'Astrea'; member of the 'fair triumvirate of wit' with Haywood and Manley
